mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and driving immediate positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when UNRATE crosses below 4.5, or negative returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and rising retail sales indicate synchronized demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue guidance, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns in any 3-year rolling window, or SPX declines exceed 10% during concurrent growth episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, an industrial production index exceeding 102 signals capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO surpasses 102 during late-cycle phases, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which translates into immediate positive momentum for the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with month-over-month HOUST increases, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 1.5 percent, borrowing costs tighten credit conditions for leveraged corporations, suppressing earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following the 1.5% threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous non-farm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and an unemployment rate below 5.0 percent confirm robust labor demand, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields a negative or flat correlation with 20-day forward returns in any 3-year rolling window, or SPX drawdowns exceed 8% during confirmed labor strength.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a retail sales figure above 450,000 million dollars signals consumer re-engagement, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during post-shock recoveries, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 4-year test period.
